Container.Name: Starr
Container.Overview: Web UI for diagnosing and repairing Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr, and Sportarr SQLite databases. Safely shuts down the target app, creates a timestamped backup, runs SQLite PRAGMA repairs (integrity check, FK repair, WAL checkpoint, VACUUM, REINDEX, ANALYZE) on the idle database, then automatically waits for the app to come back online. All repair operations are streamed live to the browser terminal. Supports dry-run mode. Backups are auto-pruned after a configurable number of days. Set SECRET_KEY to a strong random value to protect the dashboard on your network.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port for the Starr web dashboard.

Config.Name: Sonarr config
Config.Description: Sonarr config directory (must contain sonarr.db). Leave blank if not using Sonarr. Read-write is required so repairs can run.

Config.Name: Radarr config
Config.Description: Radarr config directory (must contain radarr.db). Leave blank if not using Radarr. Read-write is required so repairs can run.

Config.Name: Lidarr config
Config.Description: Lidarr config directory (must contain lidarr.db). Leave blank if not using Lidarr. Read-write is required so repairs can run.

Config.Name: Sportarr config
Config.Description: Sportarr config directory (must contain sportarr.db). Leave blank if not using Sportarr. Read-write is required so repairs can run.

Config.Name: Backups
Config.Description: Where timestamped database backups are stored. Backups are auto-pruned based on MAX_BACKUP_AGE_DAYS.

Config.Name: SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: Web UI access key — set this to a strong random value to protect the dashboard. Anyone who can reach the port can use the UI without this set.

Config.Name: PORT
Config.Description: Web UI listen port inside the container. Must match the WebUI Port mapping above.

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Log verbosity: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR.

Config.Name: MAX_BACKUP_AGE_DAYS
Config.Description: Number of days to keep backups before auto-pruning. Set to 0 to disable pruning.

Config.Name: SONARR_HOST
Config.Description: Sonarr hostname or IP (e.g. sonarr or 192.168.1.10). Leave blank to configure in the web UI.

Config.Name: SONARR_PORT
Config.Description: Sonarr HTTP port.

Config.Name: SONARR_APIKEY
Config.Description: Sonarr API key — found at Settings › General › API Key.

Config.Name: SONARR_URLBASE
Config.Description: Sonarr URL base if you use a reverse proxy subpath, e.g. /sonarr. Leave blank if none.

Config.Name: RADARR_HOST
Config.Description: Radarr hostname or IP. Leave blank to configure in the web UI.

Config.Name: RADARR_PORT
Config.Description: Radarr HTTP port.

Config.Name: RADARR_APIKEY
Config.Description: Radarr API key — found at Settings › General › API Key.

Config.Name: RADARR_URLBASE
Config.Description: Radarr URL base if using a reverse proxy subpath, e.g. /radarr. Leave blank if none.

Config.Name: LIDARR_HOST
Config.Description: Lidarr hostname or IP. Leave blank to configure in the web UI.

Config.Name: LIDARR_PORT
Config.Description: Lidarr HTTP port.

Config.Name: LIDARR_APIKEY
Config.Description: Lidarr API key — found at Settings › General › API Key.

Config.Name: LIDARR_URLBASE
Config.Description: Lidarr URL base if using a reverse proxy subpath, e.g. /lidarr. Leave blank if none.

Config.Name: SPORTARR_HOST
Config.Description: Sportarr hostname or IP. Leave blank to configure in the web UI.

Config.Name: SPORTARR_PORT
Config.Description: Sportarr HTTP port (default: 1867).

Config.Name: SPORTARR_APIKEY
Config.Description: Sportarr API key — found at Settings › General › API Key.

Config.Name: SPORTARR_URLBASE
Config.Description: Sportarr URL base if using a reverse proxy subpath, e.g. /sportarr. Leave blank if none.
